paper.title: Searching for new Hypervelocity Stars with Gaia DR3 and VLT/FORS2 Spectroscopy

extraction.summary: Extracted 17 HVS candidates from the 23 FORS2 spectroscopic targets with P_esc >= 50% under MWPotential2014. Six targets (HVS18-HVS23) with P_esc < 50% were excluded as the paper classifies them as HiVel stars rather than unbound HVSs. The remaining 126 initial candidates lack individual spectroscopic data and are not extractable.

method_chain.summary: Adopt astrometry and photometry from Gaia DR3 for sources without Gaia-provided radial velocities.

source_refs.context: Methodology section describing Gaia DR3 sample selection excluding sources with known radial velocities

method_chain.summary: Apply quality cuts: sigma_parallax/parallax <= 0.2, RUWE < 1.4, fidelity_v2 >= 0.5, parallax zero-point correction, cross-match with Gaia DR2, and astrometric fidelity checks.

source_refs.context: Quality criteria including parallax uncertainty, RUWE, and astrometric fidelity

method_chain.summary: Derive heliocentric distances from parallax inversion, checked against Bailer-Jones 2021 estimates.

source_refs.context: Distance estimation from parallax inversion

method_chain.summary: Compute 1000 estimates of total velocity v_T in the Galactic Standard of Rest frame using a multivariate Gaussian distribution, adopting a hypothetical v_los,0 that minimizes v_T.

source_refs.context: Velocity calculation methodology using multivariate Gaussian and hypothetical v_los,0

method_chain.summary: Adopt MWPotential2014 from GALPY for the primary Galactic potential, with comparisons to McMillan2017, Price-Whelan2017, bar, and LMC potentials.

source_refs.context: Adoption of MWPotential2014 for orbit integration

method_chain.summary: Compute initial escape probability P_esc,0 as the fraction of v_T realizations exceeding the local escape velocity.

source_refs.context: P_esc,0 computation as fraction of realizations where v_T > v_esc

method_chain.summary: Select 23 HVS candidates for VLT/FORS2 follow-up spectroscopy from the golden and silver subsamples, spanning a range of P_esc,0 values.

source_refs.context: Selection of 23 targets for FORS2 follow-up from golden and silver subsamples

method_chain.summary: Measure radial velocities from VLT/FORS2 spectroscopy using the Doppler code and pySME, with wavelength calibration via sky emission line at 6300.34 A.

source_refs.context: FORS2 observations and radial velocity measurement with Doppler and pySME

method_chain.summary: Recompute total Galactocentric velocities v_T using measured v_los from FORS2 together with Gaia DR3 astrometry and distances.

source_refs.context: Updated kinematics incorporating FORS2 radial velocities

method_chain.summary: Compute updated escape probability P_esc using measured v_los and MWPotential2014 escape velocity curve (primary model).

source_refs.context: P_esc computation with measured radial velocities

method_chain.summary: Integrate past orbits backward for 1 Gyr with 1 Myr time step using 1000 Monte Carlo realizations in AGAMA/GALPY.

source_refs.context: Orbit integration methodology using 1000 Monte Carlo realizations

method_chain.summary: Compute escape probabilities under alternative Galactic potential models (Price-Whelan 2017, McMillan 2017, bar, LMC-frozen, LMC-live) to assess robustness.

source_refs.context: Robustness assessment under different Galactic potential models

method_chain.summary: Classify targets by origin (extra-Galactic, disk crossing, in disk without crossing, Hills mechanism candidate) and assess robustness under alternative Galactic potentials.

source_refs.context: Origin classification scheme based on orbital properties and current position

inclusion_assessment.summary: Paper identifies HVS01 as a HVS candidate with P_esc = 100.0% under MWPotential2014, classified as 'Extra-Galactic'.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Object is listed in Table table_results with measured radial velocity and escape probability, and the paper explicitly discusses it as a HVS candidate.

source_refs.context: Paper discusses analyzed HVS candidates and their kinematic properties in Table table_results
Paper presents HVS candidates identified from Gaia DR3 and confirmed with FORS2 spectroscopy

citation_context_refs.context: Paper states HVS01, HVS02, and HVS03 were previously proposed as HVSs based on v_t only

bibliography_refs.context: Du, C., Li, H., Yan, Y., et al. 2019, ApJS, 244, 4

radial_velocity.description: Line-of-sight radial velocity from FORS2 spectroscopy

total_velocity.description: Total Galactocentric velocity in the Galactic Standard of Rest frame

galactocentric_z.description: Vertical distance from the Galactic plane

unbound_probability.description: Probability of being unbound from the Milky Way after incorporating measured radial velocity (MWPotential2014)

bound_status_metric.description: Initial probability of being unbound before follow-up spectroscopy (MWPotential2014)

spectroscopy.description: Line-of-sight radial velocity from FORS2 spectroscopy

origin_classification.description: Orbital classification based on past trajectory

hypothesis_metrics.description: Escape probability under Price-Whelan 2017 Galactic potential model
Escape probability under McMillan 2017 Galactic potential model
Escape probability under McMillan 2017 + bar Galactic potential model
Escape probability under LMC frozen Galactic potential model
Escape probability under LMC live Galactic potential model

candidate_groups_considered.description: 23 FORS2 spectroscopic targets with measured radial velocities, escape probabilities, and orbital classifications (Table table_results).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: 17 targets with P_esc >= 50% under MWPotential2014 are included as HVS candidates. Six targets (HVS18-HVS23) with P_esc < 50% are excluded as the paper classifies them as HiVel stars rather than unbound HVSs.

source_refs.context: Paper discusses kinematic results for 23 analyzed HVS candidates
Paper distinguishes confirmed HVSs from HiVel stars with P_esc <= 50%

candidate_groups_considered.description: Remaining 126 initial HVS candidates from Gaia DR3 with P_esc,0 >= 50% that were not selected for FORS2 follow-up spectroscopy.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper identifies 149 initial HVS candidates but only presents detailed kinematic data, radial velocities, and orbital analysis for 23 spectroscopic targets. The remaining 126 candidates lack the individual 6D phase-space information and bound/unbound assessment required for Stella extraction.

source_refs.context: Paper identifies 149 HVS candidates and selects 23 for spectroscopic follow-up
